message.abstract:
<jats:title>
                  A
                  <jats:sc>bstract.</jats:sc>
                </jats:title>
                <jats:p>In this paper I use the simple logistic mathematical model to represent the development of COVID-19 epidemic in São Paulo city under quarantine regime and I estimate the total amount of time it is necessary to decrease the number of seriously ill patients in order to reduce the demand for hospital beds of Intensive Care Units (ICU) to tolerable levels. Clearly the same reasoning and mathematical methods used in here can be used for any other city in similar conditions of social isolation.</jats:p>
title: TAMING COVID-19 EPIDEMIC IN SÃO PAULO WITH A LOGISTIC MODEL AND NON-PHARMACEUTICAL MEASURES

reference.unstructured: Castilho, C ., Gondim, J ., Marchesin, M.D ., Sabeti, M . Assessing the efficiency of different control strategies for the coronavirus (covid-19) epidemics. Pretprint submited to the J of Diff Eq. Also on ArXiv: http://arxiv.org/abs/2004.03539, (2020).

Fegunson, N.M . et.al. Impact of non-pharmaceutical interventions (NPIs) to reduce COVID-19 mortality and healthcare demand. Imperial College COVID-19 Response Team. March 16th, Preprint at https://mcacs.org/multimedia/files/COVID19.pdf, (2020).
Maia Martcheva , Introduction To Epidemic Modeling, An Introduction To Mathematical Epidemiology, Springer, 39, (2015).
Rocha Filho, T.M ., et al. Expected impact of COVID-19 outbreak in a major metropolitan area in Brazil. Preprint at medrxiv.org/content/10.1101/2020.03.14. (2020).

Website of the Brazilian Institute of Estatistics and Geography (IBGE) https://www.biblioteca.ibge.gov.br. Accessed on April 24th (2020).
Website Worldometter https://www.worldometers.info/coronavirus/country/brazil. Accessed throughout March and April (2020).
Website of Wikipedia: https://pt.wikipedia.org. Accessed on April 18th (2020).
Website of the city government of São Paulo: https://www.capital.sp.gov.br. Accessed on April, 18th (2020).

reference.article-title: Nowcasting and forecasting the potential domestic and international spread of the 2019-nCoV outbreak originating in Wuhan, China: a modelling study
